evidence.snippet: ##### Hybrid-Electric Engine

Half the fuel burn, double the efficiency
-----------------------------------------
The AMP-H570 sets a new standard in regional aviation, delivering breakthrough efficiency, reliability, and performance without the high costs typically associated with green technologies.

items.notes: AMP-H570 hybrid-electric engine (570 kW / 764 SHP) retrofit for existing turboprop aircraft; AmpDrive propulsion system is their core technology

evidence.snippet:
hybrid-electric.
----------------
flying today.

*   764 SHP (570 kW) at takeoff and 543 SHP (405 kW) max continuous, with electric boost providing outstanding performance
*   Fuel burn reductions of 50–70% versus traditional turboprop engines
*   Achieve 20–40% savings on direct operating costs

items.notes: Hybrid-electric power system with energy management; 50-70% fuel burn reduction claimed

items.name: Eco Otter
items.product_type: hybrid-electric Twin Otter conversion for regional/specialized operations

evidence.snippet:
Eco Otter
---------
This 1 MW aircraft is a low-emission variant of the workhorse Twin Otter turboprop, used the world over as a regional airliner, cargo hauler and bush plane, as well as in a variety of specialized operations from surveillance to parachute jumping.

Tailwind™.
----------
The Ampaire Tailwind™ embodies the future of efficient, zero emissions travel. Its clean-sheet design and next generation technology means fully-electric, fully-optimized performance in terms of range, endurance, efficiency, and speed. The Tailwind™ is the ultimate clean machine: electrically and aerodynamically. For the next generation of travelers this means more direct flights at lower costs, without the carbon footprint.

ndaa.notes: No NDAA compliance claims found anywhere in the corpus. Company makes manned aircraft, not UAS.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS claims found. Company makes manned aircraft, not UAS.

evidence.snippet: First Hybrid-Electric Engine to achieve FAA G1 certification stage: [Press Release](https://www.ampaire.com/_files/ugd/a0e619_03d4f57e5418465bb3d53ad9397aafd3.pdf)

readiness.notes: Electric EEL and Eco Caravan are described as flying 'Today'; AMP-H570 achieved FAA G1 certification stage; 30,000+ miles flown across fleet. Eco Otter is 'Tomorrow' and Tailwind is 'Beyond', suggesting a mix of early production and future development programs.

tagline: Develops hybrid-electric propulsion systems (AmpDrive) and aircraft conversions for regional aviation, claiming 30,000+ flight miles and up to 90% fuel reduction. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown. Public evidence of production readiness and defense-sector relevance is limited; claims rest primarily on commercial partnerships and research-agency engagement (NASA, ARPA-E).
